body: It will fail right now but should start passing once #161 is fixed (this test will tell us whether we actually fixed it).

labels.description: So things don't blow up and boil the lobsters alive
labels.name: numerics 🧮

labels.description: Tests get priority in case of emergency evacuation
labels.name: testing 🧪

title: Incompressibility test.

body:
This PR changes the pressure correction step to take into account the intermediate velocity field. So instead of
```
RHS[i, j, k] = div_f2c(grid, Gu, Gv, Gw, i, j, k)
```
we are using
```
RHS[i, j, k] = div_f2c(grid, u, v, w, i, j, k) / Δt + div_f2c(grid, Gu, Gv, Gw, i, j, k)
```

Resolves #161

The velocity is now actually divergence-free and does not accumulate over time:

Before:
```
[ Info: Velocity divergence after   1 time steps (CPU(), Float64): min=-1.702112707507516e-18, max=-1.702112707507516e-18, sum=8.88871484143291e-21, abs_sum=5.841423607171149e-15
[ Info: Velocity divergence after  10 time steps (CPU(), Float64): min=-6.152847328855238e-18, max=-6.152847328855238e-18, sum=3.48154736354434e-19, abs_sum=2.262474954705568e-14
[ Info: Velocity divergence after 100 time steps (CPU(), Float64): min=-4.038653092508504e-17, max=-4.038653092508504e-17, sum=3.04905391231946e-19, abs_sum=1.305988573180850e-13
```

After:
```
[ Info: Velocity divergence after   1 time steps (CPU(), Float64): min=-1.7021127075075165e-18, max=-1.7021127075075165e-18, sum=8.88871484143291e-21, abs_sum=5.841423607171149e-15
[ Info: Velocity divergence after  10 time steps (CPU(), Float64): min=-1.8295911660692887e-18, max=-1.8295911660692887e-18, sum=6.90496254158999e-20, abs_sum=6.221726243321258e-15
[ Info: Velocity divergence after 100 time steps (CPU(), Float64): min=-2.0599841277224584e-18, max=-2.0599841277224584e-18, sum=1.22291705248819e-18, abs_sum=6.683978689921446e-15
```

Well actually it seems that it still accumulates a tiny bit (much less than before though):
```
[ Info: Velocity divergence after    1 time steps (CPU(), Float64): min=-1.7021127075075165e-18, max=-1.7021127075075165e-18, sum=8.88871484143291e-21, abs_sum=5.841423607171149e-15
[ Info: Velocity divergence after   10 time steps (CPU(), Float64): min=-1.8295911660692887e-18, max=-1.8295911660692887e-18, sum=6.904962541589997e-20, abs_sum=6.221726243321258e-15
[ Info: Velocity divergence after  100 time steps (CPU(), Float64): min=-2.0599841277224584e-18, max=-2.0599841277224584e-18, sum=1.222917052488197e-18, abs_sum=6.6839786899214464e-15
[ Info: Velocity divergence after 1000 time steps (CPU(), Float64): min=-2.7755575615628914e-17, max=-2.7755575615628914e-17, sum=1.1918336233891488e-16, abs_sum=2.2196799400441293e-14
```

so maybe we also need to compute the vertical velocity _w_ from the continuity equation instead of time stepping it forward.

labels.description: Even a perfect program still has bugs
labels.name: bug 🐞

labels.description: So things don't blow up and boil the lobsters alive
labels.name: numerics 🧮

labels.description: Tests get priority in case of emergency evacuation
labels.name: testing 🧪

title: Reduce velocity divergence during time-stepping

body:
Fix Coriolis term (horizontal averaging was previous incorrect)
- remove single operator previously used for both U & V momentum Coriolis term
  since was not right
- add 2 new operators (for each horizontal momentum component) to evaluate Coriolis
  term for each horizontal momentum component.
Note: now Coriolis parameter is inside the 2 operators which should facilitate
  beta-plane version of it.
title: Fix coriolis term
